mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.5 percentage points within a single month
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or zero forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances across a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling residential housing starts indicates a structural shift in demand composition that increases earnings volatility and compresses equity multiples, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a positive coefficient in a multivariate regression with p < 0.10, or forward returns are positive in >60% of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth combined with stable consumer price inflation confirms a healthy supply-demand equilibrium that supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate exceeds 2.5% annualized
failure_condition: Forward returns average below zero when GDP growth exceeds the threshold while CPI remains below 2.0%, rejecting the equilibrium premise.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in real personal savings and durable goods consumption reflects expanding household balance sheets, which directly fuels corporate revenue and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The series exhibits positive autocorrelation but fails to predict positive equity returns in more than 50% of consecutive growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above the prevailing inflation rate, real borrowing costs turn positive and constrain corporate leverage, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds CPIAUCSL annualized inflation rate
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite real rates turning positive for three consecutive quarters, invalidating the leverage constraint mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in employment levels and unemployment rates indicate a paradoxical labor market distortion where job creation is offset by labor force expansion, increasing wage inflation pressures and compressing equity valuations over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a negative Rank IC greater than 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >55% of co-occurring events.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction-related economic activity, which transmits to broader corporate earnings and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of months where housing starts drop below the threshold, refuting the demand transmission channel.
